notes: Decision-trace evals ported from tests/scenarios/ (see RUNBOOK.md). Single rep per arm; test subjects run on sonnet for cost parity with prior rounds. Trigger tests live in trigger-eval.json and run via skill-creator's run_eval (claude -p probes).

evals.expected_output: Fixes the typo itself via Read then Edit; delegates nothing (no scout/fast-worker/deep-reasoner spawn, no peer.sh).

assertions.text: Plan contains zero delegation: no subagent spawn and no peer.sh/codex call anywhere in the proposed action sequence
Plan is a direct Read of README.md followed by an Edit replacing 'teh' with 'the' (no extra verification agents or steps beyond an optional confirmation read)
Justification does not invent extra process (no reviewer stage, no contract block) for the one-word fix

evals.expected_output: Stops delegating; escalates to the human with a neutral brief: both positions at comparable depth, crux variables, questions. No winner declared, no recommendation, no fluency/confidence/industry-prior tie-break.

assertions.text: Does not declare either schema option as the final choice, default, or recommendation (no 'I recommend', no 'default to', no lean)
Next action is escalation to the human with concrete questions about the deciding facts (tenant growth and/or compliance requirements)
Does not justify any preference by the answer's detail, confidence, or by 'industry standard/more common pattern' reasoning
Presents or plans to present both positions (shared-schema and schema-per-tenant) rather than only one

evals.expected_output: Does not read the five module files itself first; first delegation is read-only reconnaissance with a bounded, anchored return format; design step reserved for a reasoning-tier executor; dispatch carries explicit contract fields.

assertions.text: Does not propose personally reading the five auth files as the first step (no direct Read of the module files before any delegation)
First delegation is read-only reconnaissance (scout-role) whose prompt demands path:line-anchored findings
The dispatch prompt includes an explicit acceptance check and a return format (contract fields present)
Architecture/boundary design is explicitly reserved for a reasoning-tier executor (deep-reasoner/Opus), not the mechanical worker and not decided by the orchestrator before recon returns

evals.expected_output: Plans the full staged migration but writes a short handoff/ledger note to a file between stages (decisions made, alternatives rejected, risks, files touched, remaining work) and re-reads it at fan-in and before resuming after interruption; coordination state is not carried in conversation alone.

assertions.text: Plan writes a short (roughly <=20-line) handoff/ledger note to a file between stages, before moving on to the next stage
The ledger note captures decisions made, alternatives rejected, risks, files touched, and remaining work
The plan re-reads the ledger note at fan-in (when the parallel conversion batches rejoin) and/or explicitly before resuming after an interruption or compaction
No stage transition relies on conversation memory alone to carry cross-stage state — a file is the persistence mechanism

evals.expected_output: Before dispatching any design or implementation work, asks the user a small set (three or fewer) of targeted questions about the user-resolvable unknowns (IdP/protocol, treatment of existing password users, rollout/rollback constraints); does not guess the requirements or hand the ambiguity to deep-reasoner/Codex; may run a scout in parallel for repo-resolvable facts.

assertions.text: Before any design or implementation dispatch, the plan asks the user targeted questions rather than proceeding on assumptions
The number of questions asked is small and bounded (three or fewer)
The questions target genuinely user-resolvable unknowns such as IdP/protocol choice, treatment of existing password users, and rollout/rollback constraints
The plan does not delegate resolving these unknowns to deep-reasoner, Codex, or any other executor
No implementation or architecture fan-out happens before the user answers (a parallel scout dispatch for repo-resolvable facts is acceptable)

evals.expected_output: Treats the two diffs differently: runs a cheap self-check (tests/build/diff scan) for the config rename with no reviewer spawn; spawns a blind Opus-tier reviewer with a security focus for the auth overhaul despite passing tests, scoped to callers/callees, asked for a path:line defect list (not approve/reject) plus whether a meaningfully simpler approach exists.

assertions.text: For the config-rename result, the plan runs only a cheap self-check (tests/build/diff scan) and does not spawn a reviewer
For the auth-overhaul result, the plan spawns a blind reviewer despite the report that tests pass
The auth-overhaul reviewer is pinned to the Opus tier and given an explicit security focus
The auth-overhaul reviewer is asked to return a path:line defect list, not an approve/reject verdict
The auth-overhaul reviewer's scope includes callers/callees of the changed code and/or is asked whether a meaningfully simpler approach exists
